title: Are Video Games Worth It?
content:
NOTE: Please excuse my bad writing skills.

Let's be real ...

Are games worth it? I have been asking myself this question for a very long time. I have switched the "games" page on this website to "projects" because I honestly don't think they are worth my time. Now, before you get mad at me, let me explain.

The issue: designed to be addictive?

In my prime (or my lowest?) I was a pretty hardcore gamer. I racked up 500 hours on Hearts of Iron IV in around 6 months. I'm ashamed to admit that I have probably spent thousands of hours on Minecraft, Roblox, Clash Royale, and Brawl Stars, and I have nothing to show for it. Well, was it at least fun? Not really. Brawl Stars and Clash Royale are designed to keep you hooked. You receive daily rewards for playing. You are punished for not playing because you fall behind other players. Eventually, you're just logging on and playing not because you want to but because you don't want to fall behind other players. It's not just Supercell games. A lot of Minecraft is just collecting materials for hours. I would just be thinking, "What am I doing with my life?" as I mind-numbingly chopped down trees or mined stone. I wasn't having fun, just playing. Games are addictive because they make progress so easy. It's easier to progress in a game than it is in real life. To keep that feeling of progressing, we keep playing even when we don't really want to. Developers hijack this natural human instinct and use it to make games more addictive. After a while, the game becomes a chore and not what it was supposed to be: fun. Then, you get bored, start a new game, and the cycle repeats.

Other side effects

You are the result of what you consume. If you consume a lot of video games, they will begin to consume your life. This is a side effect that I think most people don't notice. Playing video games hijacks your thoughts. You begin thinking about the game even when you're not playing it. I know I used to do this a lot. I would think about things I wanted to build in Minecraft or strategies for Hearts of Iron IV. It takes away your brain power and leaves you less clarity to actually think about the things that matter. After playing for a long time, you feel like a zombie.

My final verdict

I'm not going to say that all video games are bad. I did have some good times playing them. What I will say is that they need to be played in moderation. Playing 8 hours a day isn't healthy. 1 to 2 hours a day is probably the sweet spot. Play games that respect your time and aren't designed to be addictive. If you have trouble moderating the amount of time you play games, then it is okay to quit. Video game addiction is a real thing. Personally, I found it hard to moderate, so I just quit entirely. I just want to move on from that point in my life where I wasted a lot of time just playing games. That is why I have changed the "games" tab to "projects."

Thank you for reading.

title: New Blog Features
content:
I created this post to experiement and show off all the cool things I can do on this blog. Right now I am using a callout.

I can now use headers, pretty cool

I can use both inline text and

this kind of text

I can also use links inside of my text like here. My favorite part is that I can now use images.

IMPORTANT

This cat is very cute

I'm pretty happy with how these turned out. I also updated my previous posts. Thank you for reading.

title: My Blogs Are Now Loaded Dynamically
content:
All of these blog pages are now loaded dynamically with Javascript. This is the first time that I have used Js on this website. I can't lie, I created this post just to test out the Js. It's nice because now I can just use one singular json file and not have to worry about creating a HTML file for every post. There are still a few things that need to be worked out but it's fine for the most part. I'm pretty happy with how this turned out. The next step for me will be to add images, headers, and code blocks into the blog system.


Thank you for reading.

NOTE: This post has been updated.

title: First/Test Blog Post
content:
Why I created this website

I'm not really sure how to manage a website but I guess I’ll learn as I go. I created this website because I got rejected from a lot of colleges and I realized that I need a place to display my work and develop my website making skills. I’m also going to work on this blog to try and improve my writing skills. I have never really been a great writer so maybe this blog can change that. I’ll update this blog when I feel like something interesting has happened.


Whats next

For this blog system, I heard that php and next.js make this process easier, but I've been told that Github only allows static websites. So for now, I'll just stick to the basics. I’m going to work on loading content with Javascript rather than making an HTML page every time I need to display something. It will be much easier to maintain.


Thank you for reading.

NOTE: This post has been updated.
